about.name: Felipe Caceres - Fernandez
about.bio: Hey my name is Felipe and I'm working towards making a difference in the world through robotics, specifically humanoid robotics. I'm an undergrad in Computational Physics at Carnegie Mellon University. Physics teaches you to reason about how the world works from first principles and computer science trains you to analyze problems with math, logic, and abstraction. I'm interested in humanoid robotics because they will have the highest capability of almost anything humans ever create and it forces you to understand different technical fields and to solve complex problems.

experience.org: Origami Robotics (Humanoid Robotics Startup, YC W26)
experience.description: Owned full stack development of tactile sensing.

experience.org: Amazon (LEO, mass satellite constellation for global internet)
experience.description: Modeling new OCV-SOC and capacity degradation data from internal cell testing to improve algorithm for battery state estimation.

projects.title: 2022 Intake Mechanism (FRC 1678)
projects.description: 4-bar linkage mechanism with powered rollers and actuation
projects.detail: A from-scratch simulator for articulated robot arms, integrating rigid-body dynamics, contact resolution, and joint limits in a configurable control loop. Built to test motion-planning and control ideas without risking real hardware. Every force, constraint, and integration step is inspectable and logged.
